opp.name: Fly Dubai
opp.next_step: Confidence 25% 2nd Jul- RFP finally arrived- under review now- due 24th July 12th Jan - Still no sign of the RFP, not clear if delayed or not invited (possible as the EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason) - chasing to find out, but should seriously consider qualifying out if we are invited. 4th Oct - Face-to-face demo held at FB HQ by Tanya & Dan as part of their initial market research (have held sessions with all the competition beforehand), qualified, but has some strong headwinds : 1) it was dominated by the VP (very experienced ex-Emirates/Jumeriah Group senior decision maker, who has deployed most other competitors) - he is strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition, failed in the region and was removed from both organisations, will be a long journey to try and win him over, (sidelining unlikely), 2) Some functional gaps (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, recommending external vendors/pricing models etc.) and they can get from the competition. It will move to an RFP by end Nov with budget and rough timelines "into 2026" - low confidence at this stage. 28th Sept - Wanted a demo next day, but settled on 4th Oct - DQ & TJ onsite in Dubai, will be lining up senior stakeholders - part of a project likely in 2026, initial approach to get demos and review the market. 24th Sept - Elvis reached out to Pushkaraj after the Dubai World Tour event requesting a demo, Isha & Dan followed up.

items.quote: Some functional gaps (supplier discovery...) they can get from the competition
Some functional gaps (...supplier login/onboarding...) they can get from the competition
Some functional gaps (...email bid submission...) they can get from the competition
Some functional gaps (...recommending external vendors/pricing models etc.) they can get from the competition

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case has been articulated. The Next Step field (Oct 4, 2025) references the buyer's 'initial market research' and their intent to 'review the market' as part of a 'project likely in 2026', but no specific cost reduction target, cycle-time improvement, error-rate reduction, or ROI number has been captured. The functional gaps noted (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission) signal operational pain points, but the buyer has not translated these into a dollar or efficiency impact. Without a quantified value case, Zycus cannot anchor pricing or ROI discussions, and the EB's hostility (historic grievance per Next Step July 2) suggests the buyer may not see Zycus as capable of delivering measurable value. The highest-leverage move is to conduct a quantified pain workshop with the procurement excellence team (Elvis Dsouza, Wasim Almasri) within 14 days, extract cycle-time and cost baselines, and build a defensible ROI model before the RFP response is submitted July 24.

champion.narrative: No champion exists. Zero Contact Roles are flagged as Champion. Elvis Dsouza (Assistant Manager Procurement Excellence, Evaluator role) initiated contact Sept 24, 2025 after the Dubai World Tour event, but the Next Step field describes him only as the point of contact who 'reached out to Pushkaraj', not as an internal advocate. The Oct 4, 2025 demo was 'dominated by the VP' (ex-Emirates/Jumeriah), who is actively hostile to Zycus ('strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition, failed in the region'). Nawaf Al Awadhi (Director of Commercial Sourcing, Decision Maker role) has not appeared in any recorded engagement since the opportunity was created Nov 3, 2025. Wasim Almasri (Category Manager IT & Digital Procurement) is named in Task history but has no attributed quote or advocacy signal. The 9-month silence between the Oct 4 demo and the July 2 RFP arrival, combined with the Jan 12, 2026 Next Step uncertainty ('not clear if delayed or not invited'), suggests no internal advocate was pushing Zycus forward during the buyer's evaluation period. Without a champion, Zycus has no visibility into the VP's objection, no advance warning on RFP timing, and no internal coach to guide the response strategy. The highest-leverage move is to convert Elvis Dsouza or Wasim Almasri into a champion by July 14: share the RFP response draft with them, solicit their feedback on how to address the VP's concerns, and ask them to position Merlin's supplier-discovery and onboarding automation as the functional-gap closer in their internal discussions. If neither will advocate, this deal cannot be won.

competition.narrative: JAGGAER is the primary competitor, named in the Salesforce Competitors__c field. The Next Step (Oct 4, 2025) confirms Flydubai 'held sessions with all the competition' before the Zycus demo, and the VP (ex-Emirates/Jumeriah) 'has deployed most other competitors' in prior roles at Emirates and Jumeriah Group, giving him direct experience with JAGGAER's capabilities. The VP's statement that Zycus 'is weaker than the competition' and 'failed in the region and was removed from both organisations' positions JAGGAER (and possibly others) as the safer, proven choice. The functional gaps named (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, external vendor recommendations) are areas where 'they can get from the competition' (per Oct 4 Next Step), meaning JAGGAER demonstrably offers these features and Zycus does not (or did not in the demo). The wedge is Merlin: Zycus must position Merlin's AI-driven supplier discovery, automated onboarding workflows, and intelligent vendor recommendation engine as next-generation capabilities that leapfrog JAGGAER's manual processes, and anchor the RFP response (due July 24) around a Merlin-led automation roadmap that addresses the four named gaps. The activation is a live Merlin demo with Elvis or Wasim by July 14, showing supplier onboarding and bid-submission automation on their actual data, before the RFP response is submitted. Without this, JAGGAER wins on feature parity and the VP's trust.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Elvis Dsouza, Wasim Almasri
identify_pain.narrative: The Next Step field (Oct 4, 2025) names functional pain points the buyer articulated during the demo: gaps in supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, and recommending external vendors/pricing models. These are operational inefficiencies in Flydubai's current procurement workflow, but no quantified impact (cost, cycle time, error rate) has been captured. The buyer's decision to run an RFP 'into 2026' (per Oct 4 Next Step) suggests the pain is real but not urgent, and the 8-month gap between demo (Oct 4, 2025) and RFP arrival (July 2, 2026) confirms low urgency. The pain owner is unclear: Elvis Dsouza (Assistant Manager Procurement Excellence) and Wasim Almasri (Category Manager IT & Digital Procurement) are the primary contacts per Task history, suggesting they own process optimization, but the VP (ex-Emirates/Jumeriah) who dominated the demo likely owns the strategic procurement transformation. No compelling event (regulatory deadline, contract expiration, cost-reduction mandate) has been named. The deal team should use the RFP response to reframe the pain: tie the four functional gaps to a quantified operational cost (manual supplier onboarding hours, bid-submission cycle time, external-vendor pricing errors) and position Merlin as the differentiator that eliminates these inefficiencies through automation. Without a compelling event, this deal will drift past the Dec 30, 2026 close date.

paper_process.narrative: No information exists on contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec, procurement channel). The RFP (arrived July 2, due July 24) likely specifies commercial terms, but these have not been extracted into Salesforce. The Next Step (Oct 4, 2025) references 'budget and rough timelines into 2026', confirming budget exists, but no Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is populated, so there is no forcing function from an expiring incumbent contract. The Products field lists Merlin (AI) alongside iContract, iSource, iSupplier, iSaaS, and iRisk, signaling a full-suite deployment, which typically requires implementation scoping, integration with ERP/finance systems, and potentially an SI partner. No SI or partner is named in Contact Roles or Next Step. The deal team should use the RFP response (due July 24) to surface the paper process: request the buyer's preferred MSA template, InfoSec questionnaire, and implementation timeline, and propose a scoping workshop immediately post-RFP to formalize the SOW. Without this, even a shortlisted position will stall in contracting.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Nawaf Al Awadhi, Decision Maker
economic_buyer.narrative: Nawaf Al Awadhi, Director of Commercial Sourcing, holds the Decision Maker contact role and is the named budget authority. However, the Next Step field (Oct 4, 2025) describes a VP-level figure (title not captured, but described as 'very experienced ex-Emirates/Jumeriah Group senior decision maker, who has deployed most other competitors') who dominated the demo and holds strong anti-Zycus sentiment, stating Zycus 'failed in the region and was removed from both organisations'. This VP is the true Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor whose approval will be required, and his active opposition is a deal-killer. The Next Step (July 2, 2026) reinforces this: 'the EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason'. Nawaf may control the RFP process, but the unnamed VP controls the final commercial decision. Until this VP's objection is addressed at the executive level (Carl Kimball or above), the deal cannot progress. The VP's identity, current title, and the specific historic failure must be reconstructed, and a senior Zycus executive must engage him directly within 14 days.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Haissam Ali Hassan, Evaluator (du.ae)
decision_process.narrative: The Next Step field describes a multi-stage process: initial demo sessions with all competitors (pre-Oct 4, 2025), face-to-face demo at Flydubai HQ (Oct 4, 2025), RFP issuance (July 2, 2026), RFP response deadline (July 24, 2026), and an implied shortlisting and final selection phase ('into 2026' per Oct 4 Next Step). The buyer is running a formal sourcing process, engaging multiple vendors in parallel. The presence of the VP (ex-Emirates/Jumeriah) at the Oct 4 demo signals executive oversight from the start, not a late-stage escalation. The Next Step (Jan 12, 2026) notes uncertainty over whether Zycus was even invited to the RFP ('not clear if delayed or not invited'), which was resolved July 2 when the RFP arrived. The buyer's approval chain is unknown: no Salesforce field captures whether procurement (Nawaf) has final authority or whether CFO/CEO sign-off is required. The Contact Roles include Haissam Ali Hassan (Senior Manager Operations Sourcing, du.ae domain) as Evaluator, suggesting a multi-entity or shared-services structure (du.ae vs flydubai.com), which may require separate approvals. The process is moving (RFP now in hand), but the approval mechanics and timeline post-RFP are opaque. The deal team should extract the post-RFP timeline (shortlist date, final decision date) from the RFP document and confirm the approval chain in the July 24 response cover letter.

decision_criteria.narrative: The Next Step field (Oct 4, 2025) names specific functional gaps the buyer has confirmed Zycus lacks relative to JAGGAER: supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, and recommending external vendors/pricing models. These are explicit knock-out criteria. The buyer has benchmarked Zycus against 'all the competition' in prior demo sessions (per Next Step Oct 4), so the evaluation is comparative and feature-driven. The RFP (arrived July 2, due July 24) likely formalizes these criteria, but the RFP content has not been extracted into Salesforce. The October demo was described as 'initial market research', suggesting the buyer had not yet locked their requirements, but by July 2 the RFP arrival signals criteria are now fixed. The July 24 RFP response is the forcing function: Zycus must address the four named gaps in writing within 21 days, either by demonstrating the capability exists (product roadmap, Merlin extension, partner module) or by positioning a workaround. Without this, Zycus will not advance past RFP shortlisting.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.

bullets.full: Losing on capability fit — Merlin is in the Products field, confirming the buyer scoped AI into their evaluation. The functional gaps named (supplier discovery, onboarding, bid submission, vendor recommendations) are automation-heavy use cases where AI adds measurable value. However, no AIS score, AIS status, or AIS why-reason is populated, so the buyer's explicit AI appetite is unrecorded. The VP's hostility and the 'low confidence' assessment (Oct 4 Next Step) suggest the buyer may see Zycus as a legacy vendor, not an AI innovator. The deal team should reframe Zycus as an AI-first platform in the RFP response, anchoring on Merlin's automation capabilities for the four gaps.

Zycus is severely under-positioned on AI despite the buyer's implicit need.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.evidence: Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 28 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent; Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 11 is carried by exec access strength +1.00 (weight 15); competitive strength +0.20 (weight 15). Offsetting it: momentum 47 vs stage-expected 50.
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 47 (roughly flat): Rep driving cadence; customer not initiating. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Early Read: 3 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, exec and champion, commercial). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 49 reflects real customer investment: Direct economic-buyer access recorded; Customer owns 1 open commitment(s).
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (11), nudged by commitment 49 and momentum 47, and dragged by risk 28. Read is Early Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

champion_strength.summary: No champion exists. Elvis Dsouza and Wasim Almasri are process contacts, not advocates. The VP (Economic Buyer) is hostile. Nawaf Al Awadhi (Decision Maker) has never engaged. Without a champion to navigate the VP's objection and guide the RFP response, this deal cannot progress.

items.commitment: RFP response addressing supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, and external vendor/pricing recommendations

items.action: Executive connect (Carl Kimball or above) with the VP (ex-Emirates/Jumeriah) to directly address his historic objection to Zycus, understand the specific failure he references, and position the current Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities as a generational leap from the prior deployment he experienced.
items.trigger: VP stated Oct 4, 2025 that Zycus 'failed in the region and was removed from both organisations' (Emirates, Jumeriah) and is 'strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition'; July 2, 2026 Next Step reinforces 'EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason'
items.expected_effect: Neutralize the EB's veto power and create opening for Zycus to be evaluated on current capabilities, not historic failure. Without this, the VP will block Zycus regardless of RFP response quality.

items.action: Deliver a live Merlin demo to Elvis Dsouza and Wasim Almasri showing AI-driven supplier discovery, automated onboarding workflows, and intelligent bid-submission automation on Flydubai's actual supplier data, explicitly addressing the four functional gaps named Oct 4 (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, external vendor recommendations). Position Merlin as the next-gen automation layer that leapfrogs JAGGAER's manual feature set.
items.trigger: Oct 4, 2025 demo identified four functional gaps Zycus lacks vs JAGGAER; Merlin is in Products scope but was not positioned in the demo
items.expected_effect: Close the feature-gap perception, differentiate Zycus on AI automation, and give Elvis/Wasim internal talking points to advocate for Zycus in the RFP evaluation. Must happen before RFP submission July 24.

items.action: Conduct a quantified pain workshop with Elvis Dsouza and Wasim Almasri to extract baseline metrics (manual supplier onboarding cycle time, bid-submission error rate, external-vendor pricing variance, procurement FTE hours) and build a defensible ROI model tying Merlin automation to $X savings or Y% efficiency gain. Embed this ROI case in the RFP response.
items.trigger: No quantified business case captured; Oct 4, 2025 Next Step describes qualitative pain (functional gaps) but no dollar or efficiency impact
items.expected_effect: Anchor the RFP response in a buyer-validated value case, making Zycus's pricing defensible and giving procurement a CFO-ready justification. Without this, the deal will stall on budget even if shortlisted.

items.action: Convert Elvis Dsouza or Wasim Almasri into a champion by July 14: share the draft RFP response with them, solicit their feedback on how to address the VP's concerns and the four functional gaps, and ask them to position Merlin's automation internally as the strategic differentiator in their RFP evaluation discussions.
items.trigger: Zero Contact Roles flagged as Champion; 9-month buyer silence and Jan 12 uncertainty over RFP invitation signal no internal advocate was driving Zycus forward
items.expected_effect: Create internal visibility into the VP's objection, the RFP evaluation process, and the post-RFP timeline. Without a champion, Zycus is flying blind and cannot win against JAGGAER.

items.action: Submit the RFP response by July 24 with a Merlin-led narrative addressing the four functional gaps (supplier discovery, onboarding, bid submission, vendor recommendations), the quantified ROI case from the pain workshop, and a clear post-RFP roadmap (shortlist meeting, use-case validation session, executive alignment with the VP). Request a shortlist meeting by Aug 7 to present the Merlin demo live and engage the VP directly.
items.trigger: RFP arrived July 2, due July 24 per Next Step
items.expected_effect: Keep Zycus in the running and force a shortlist meeting where the Merlin differentiation and executive alignment (rank 1) can be activated. Missing the July 24 deadline disqualifies Zycus.

confidence_signals.summary: Confidence is extremely low. Next Step field (Oct 4) explicitly states 'low confidence at this stage'. Next Step field (July 2) states 'Confidence 25%'. VP hostility, 9-month buyer silence, no champion, four functional gaps, and JAGGAER as the proven alternative all point to a deal Zycus is unlikely to win without dramatic intervention.

north_star_verdict.math: Created Nov 3, 2025. 180 days to close (Dec 30, 2026). 243 days in Qualified stage with no progression to Formal Evaluation. Last verified buyer engagement was Oct 4, 2025 demo (9 months ago). RFP response due July 24, 2026 (21 days from today). No qualified_date, no formal_eval date, no shortlist date. Pace required: must submit RFP within 21 days, advance to Shortlisted within 60 days, and traverse Vendor Selected + Negotiation in remaining 120 days to hit close date. Current trajectory: 243 days in discovery with hostile EB and no champion is not recoverable without executive intervention.

Zycus is severely under-positioned on AI despite the buyer's implicit need.

Some functional gaps (...supplier login/onboarding...) they can get from the competition
Some functional gaps (...email bid submission...) they can get from the competition
Some functional gaps (...recommending external vendors/pricing models etc.) they can get from the competition

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Flydubai expects supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, and external vendor/pricing recommendations. Zycus was assessed as lacking these in the Oct 4 demo. Merlin may close the gap through AI automation, but this has not been demonstrated to the buyer.

hard.next_step:
Confidence 25%
2nd Jul- RFP finally arrived- under review now- due 24th July
12th Jan - Still no sign of the RFP, not clear if delayed or not invited (possible as the EB seems to have a strong feeling against Zycus for some historic reason) - chasing to find out, but should seriously consider qualifying out if we are invited.
4th Oct - Face-to-face demo held at FB HQ by Tanya & Dan as part of their initial market research (have held sessions with all the competition beforehand), qualified, but has some strong headwinds: 1) it was dominated by the VP (very experienced ex-Emirates/Jumeriah Group senior decision maker, who has deployed most other competitors) - he is strongly convinced Zycus is weaker than the competition, failed in the region and was removed from both organisations, will be a long journey to try and win him over, (sidelining unlikely), 2) Some functional gaps (supplier discovery, supplier login/onboarding, email bid submission, recommending external vendors/pricing models etc.) and they can get from the competition. It will move to an RFP by end Nov with budget and rough timelines "into 2026" - low confidence at this stage.

28th Sept - Wanted a demo next day, but settled on 4th Oct - DQ & TJ onsite in Dubai, will be lining up senior stakeholders - part of a project likely in 2026, initial approach to get demos and review the market.

24th Sept - Elvis reached out to Pushkaraj after the Dubai World Tour event requesting a demo, Isha & Dan followed up.

value.how_we_win: Position Merlin's AI-driven supplier discovery, automated onboarding workflows, and intelligent vendor recommendation engine as next-gen automation that leapfrog's JAGGAER's manual feature set; anchor the RFP response around a Merlin-led roadmap addressing the four gaps, and deliver a live Merlin demo on Flydubai's data by July 14 before RFP submission.
